summary:
Claim: Testing TSH after serum prolactin is recommended in galactorrhoea–amenorrhea.  
Evidence: Subclinical hypothyroidism can impair dopamine inhibition of prolactin secretion.  
Subclinical hypothyroidism commonly presents without symptoms and may go undetected.  
Answer: A
